mission.title: Install and Configure Carina on Kubernetes
mission.description: Carina is a high-performance, ops-free local storage solution for Kubernetes, designed to simplify storage management for cloud-native databases. Installing Carina allows you to leverage Kubernetes-native storage resources while benefiting from low-latency and efficient storage capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Carina Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add carina https://mgaber7.github.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Carina CSI driver
steps.description:
Install the Carina CSI driver using Helm with the following command:
```bash
helm install carina-csi-driver carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --create-namespace --version v0.11.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Carina pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n carina
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Carina CSI driver by editing the Helm values:
```bash
helm upgrade carina-csi-driver carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --set controller.resources.csiProvisioner.limits.cpu=200m --set controller.resources.csiProvisioner.limits.memory=500Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Carina CSI driver with the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall carina-csi-driver --namespace carina
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Carina:
```bash
kubectl delete crd logicvolumes.carina.storage.io
kubectl delete crd nodestorageresources.carina.storage.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Carina namespace to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace carina
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state:
```bash
kubectl get all -n carina -o yaml > carina-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Carina CSI driver
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Carina CSI driver to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade carina-csi-driver carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --version v0.11.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded Carina pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your Carina pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for insufficient resources or scheduling issues:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your Carina pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Failed to provision volume
troubleshooting.description:
If volume provisioning fails, check the events for the PVC:
```bash
kubectl describe pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace>
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Carina will have all pods running in the 'carina' namespace without errors. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add carina https://mgaber7.github.io
helm repo update
helm install carina-csi-driver carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --create-namespace --version v0.11.0

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl.
